kata.title: Valid Sudoku
kata.workload: TOTAL=5M single-pass bitmask validations of a perturbed fully-solved 9x9 board (perturb-validate-restore), verdict folded into a checksum

kata.title: Sudoku Solver
kata.workload: TOTAL=500 bitmask-backtracking solves of the world's hardest sudoku (Inkala 2012) with cell k%81 cleared per iteration, solved grid folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: Count and Say
kata.workload: TOTAL=12000 generalized count-and-say runs (seed = decimal of k+1, STEPS=14 streaming run-length say steps, single-digit run counts appended in place), final term folded into a position-weighted digit checksum

kata.title: Combination Sum
kata.workload: TOTAL=30000 combination-sum enumerations (candidates [2,3,5,7], target = 18 + k%13), mutable-path backtracking with a leaf path.clone() into Vec[Vec[i64]], every combination folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: Combination Sum II
kata.workload: TOTAL=30000 combination-sum-II enumerations (duplicate-heavy multiset [1,1,2,2,3,3,4,5,6,7] sorted once, target = 10 + k%13), sorted same-level-dedup backtracking (each element used once) with a leaf path.clone() into Vec[Vec[i64]], every combination folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: First Missing Positive
kata.workload: TOTAL=200000 first-missing-positive solves on a reused length-100 buffer (refilled each iteration with a k-rotated permutation of 1..100 plus one punched gap), in-place cyclic-sort (swap each value to its home index v-1, then scan), each answer folded into a rolling checksum

kata.title: Trapping Rain Water
kata.workload: TOTAL=200000 trapping-rain-water solves on a length-1000 terrain ((i*37)%100) built once, with one slot punched per iteration (height[k%n]=(k*19)%100) to shift the basins, converging two-pointer (advance the shorter outer wall, settle each column with its running max), each answer folded into a rolling checksum

kata.title: Multiply Strings
kata.workload: TOTAL=300K product-strings (38-digit operand × decimal(k)) concatenated + byte-checksum

kata.title: Wildcard Matching
kata.workload: TOTAL=200000 wildcard-matching solves on a length-1000 terrain ((i*37)%100) built once, with one slot punched per iteration (height[k%n]=(k*19)%100) to shift the basins, converging two-pointer (advance the shorter outer wall, settle each column with its running max), each answer folded into a rolling checksum

kata.title: Jump Game II
kata.workload: TOTAL=200000 minimum-jumps solves on a length-1000 reachable array (entries 1..4, built once) with one slot punched per iteration (nums[k%n]=1+k%9) to widen a reach and shift the jump count, greedy range expansion (one cursor; farthest = running max of i+nums[i], spend a jump and extend current_end on reaching the layer boundary), each answer folded into a rolling checksum

kata.title: Permutations
kata.workload: TOTAL=300 permutation enumerations of a fixed n=7 array (5040 perms each, one slot punched per iteration nums[k%n]=1+k%9, not reverted), used-array mutable-path backtracking with a leaf path.clone() into Vec[Vec[i64]], every permutation folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: Permutations II
kata.workload: TOTAL=600 unique-permutation enumerations of a fixed n=8 multiset from {1,2,3,4} (one slot punched per iteration nums[k%n]=1+k%4, not reverted), sorted used-array adjacent-skip backtracking with a leaf path.clone() into Vec[Vec[i64]], every permutation folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: Rotate Image
kata.workload: TOTAL=40000 in-place 90° rotations of a fixed n=20 matrix (one cell punched per iteration m[k%n][(k*7)%n]=k%97, not reverted), layer four-way cyclic swap via a four-target parallel assignment, every cell folded into a position-weighted checksum

kata.title: Group Anagrams
kata.workload: sorted-key grouping: N=20_000 words (L=8), 26 classes, K=40 outer iters

kata.title: Maximum Subarray

kata.title: Spiral Matrix
kata.workload: Boundary-shrinking spiral over a batch of 200000 LCG-filled 24x24 matrices, i64 sink of per-matrix position-weighted checksums

kata.title: Jump Game
kata.workload: TOTAL=200000 reachability decisions on a length-1000 reachable array (entries 1..4, built once) with one slot punched per iteration (nums[k%n]=1+k%9) to widen a reach, greedy forward max-reach (one cursor; farthest = running max of i+nums[i], decide the moment farthest covers the last index), the decision index folded into a rolling checksum

env.note: Measured on the corpus's Apple M5 Pro (arm64) — the canonical host. The earlier x86-64 Linux cloud-container run is preserved verbatim in results.container-x86.json for anyone re-running a regression on that host; its absolute times/sizes/RSS are NOT comparable here (different ISA + toolchains). RESOLUTION of the container's flagged RC gap: the container measured kara RC ~1.09x behind Rust Rc and flagged it for the M5 re-bench before treating as a tracked regression — on the M5 that gap VANISHES. kara RC 237.8ms vs equal-semantics Rust Rc 235.7ms is a statistical tie (1.009x, overlapping sigma); kara's entire +7% over Rust Box (222.2ms) is the RC-vs-unique-ownership cost that Rust itself pays going Box->Rc (+6%), not kara codegen overhead. Machine flip worth noting: Go went from slowest on the container (527ms, GC) to FASTEST on M5 (163.2ms) — its bump allocator + GC wins on fast cores for this build-heavy tree workload. No tracked regression.
